tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceAssociateDelegateToResource.associateDelegateToResource
tools.description: Adds a member (user or group) to the resource's set of delegates.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceAssociateMemberToGroup.associateMemberToGroup
tools.description: Adds a member (user or group) to the group's set.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceAssumeImpersonationRole.assumeImpersonationRole
tools.description: Assumes an impersonation role for the given WorkMail organization. This method returns an authentication token you can use to make impersonated calls.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceCancelMailboxExportJob.cancelMailboxExportJob
tools.description:
Cancels a mailbox export job.

If the mailbox export job is near completion, it might not be possible to cancel it.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceCreateAlias.createAlias
tools.description: Adds an alias to the set of a given member (user or group) of WorkMail.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceCreateAvailabilityConfiguration.createAvailabilityConfiguration
tools.description: Creates an AvailabilityConfiguration for the given WorkMail organization and domain.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceCreateGroup.createGroup
tools.description: Creates a group that can be used in WorkMail by calling the RegisterToWorkMail operation.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceCreateImpersonationRole.createImpersonationRole
tools.description:
Creates an impersonation role for the given WorkMail organization.

Idempotency ensures that an API request completes no more than one time. With an idempotent request, if the original request completes successfully, any subsequent retries also complete successfully without performing any further actions.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceCreateMobileDeviceAccessRule.createMobileDeviceAccessRule
tools.description: Creates a new mobile device access rule for the specified WorkMail organization.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceCreateOrganization.createOrganization
tools.description:
Creates a new WorkMail organization. Optionally, you can choose to associate an existing AWS Directory Service directory with your organization. If an AWS Directory Service directory ID is specified, the organization alias must match the directory alias. If you choose not to associate an existing directory with your organization, then we create a new WorkMail directory for you. For more information, see Adding an organization in the WorkMail Administrator Guide.

You can associate multiple email domains with an organization, then choose your default email domain from the WorkMail console. You can also associate a domain that is managed in an Amazon Route 53 public hosted zone. For more information, see Adding a domain and Choosing the default domain in the WorkMail Administrator Guide.

Optionally, you can use a customer managed key from AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) to encrypt email for your organization. If you don't associate an AWS KMS key, WorkMail creates a default, AWS managed key for you.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceCreateResource.createResource
tools.description: Creates a new WorkMail resource.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceCreateUser.createUser
tools.description: Creates a user who can be used in WorkMail by calling the RegisterToWorkMail operation.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceDeleteAccessControlRule.deleteAccessControlRule
tools.description:
Deletes an access control rule for the specified WorkMail organization.

Deleting already deleted and non-existing rules does not produce an error. In those cases, the service sends back an HTTP 200 response with an empty HTTP body.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceDeleteAlias.deleteAlias
tools.description: Remove one or more specified aliases from a set of aliases for a given user.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceDeleteAvailabilityConfiguration.deleteAvailabilityConfiguration
tools.description: Deletes the AvailabilityConfiguration for the given WorkMail organization and domain.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceDeleteEmailMonitoringConfiguration.deleteEmailMonitoringConfiguration
tools.description: Deletes the email monitoring configuration for a specified organization.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceDeleteGroup.deleteGroup
tools.description: Deletes a group from WorkMail.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceDeleteImpersonationRole.deleteImpersonationRole
tools.description: Deletes an impersonation role for the given WorkMail organization.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceDeleteMailboxPermissions.deleteMailboxPermissions
tools.description: Deletes permissions granted to a member (user or group).

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceDeleteMobileDeviceAccessOverride.deleteMobileDeviceAccessOverride
tools.description:
Deletes the mobile device access override for the given WorkMail organization, user, and device.

Deleting already deleted and non-existing overrides does not produce an error. In those cases, the service sends back an HTTP 200 response with an empty HTTP body.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceDeleteMobileDeviceAccessRule.deleteMobileDeviceAccessRule
tools.description:
Deletes a mobile device access rule for the specified WorkMail organization.

Deleting already deleted and non-existing rules does not produce an error. In those cases, the service sends back an HTTP 200 response with an empty HTTP body.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceDeleteOrganization.deleteOrganization
tools.description: Deletes an WorkMail organization and all underlying AWS resources managed by WorkMail as part of the organization. You can choose whether to delete the associated directory. For more information, see Removing an organization in the WorkMail Administrator Guide.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceDeleteResource.deleteResource
tools.description: Deletes the specified resource.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceDeleteRetentionPolicy.deleteRetentionPolicy
tools.description: Deletes the specified retention policy from the specified organization.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceDeleteUser.deleteUser
tools.description:
Deletes a user from WorkMail and all subsequent systems. Before you can delete a user, the user state must be DISABLED. Use the DescribeUser action to confirm the user state.

Deleting a user is permanent and cannot be undone. WorkMail archives user mailboxes for 30 days before they are permanently removed.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceDeregisterFromWorkMail.deregisterFromWorkMail
tools.description: Mark a user, group, or resource as no longer used in WorkMail. This action disassociates the mailbox and schedules it for clean-up. WorkMail keeps mailboxes for 30 days before they are permanently removed. The functionality in the console is Disable.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceDeregisterMailDomain.deregisterMailDomain
tools.description: Removes a domain from WorkMail, stops email routing to WorkMail, and removes the authorization allowing WorkMail use. SES keeps the domain because other applications may use it. You must first remove any email address used by WorkMail entities before you remove the domain.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceDescribeEmailMonitoringConfiguration.describeEmailMonitoringConfiguration
tools.description: Describes the current email monitoring configuration for a specified organization.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceDescribeGroup.describeGroup
tools.description: Returns the data available for the group.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceDescribeInboundDmarcSettings.describeInboundDmarcSettings
tools.description: Lists the settings in a DMARC policy for a specified organization.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceDescribeMailboxExportJob.describeMailboxExportJob
tools.description: Describes the current status of a mailbox export job.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceDescribeOrganization.describeOrganization
tools.description: Provides more information regarding a given organization based on its identifier.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceDescribeResource.describeResource
tools.description: Returns the data available for the resource.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceDescribeUser.describeUser
tools.description: Provides information regarding the user.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceDisassociateDelegateFromResource.disassociateDelegateFromResource
tools.description: Removes a member from the resource's set of delegates.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceDisassociateMemberFromGroup.disassociateMemberFromGroup
tools.description: Removes a member from a group.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceGetAccessControlEffect.getAccessControlEffect
tools.description: Gets the effects of an organization's access control rules as they apply to a specified IPv4 address, access protocol action, and user ID or impersonation role ID. You must provide either the user ID or impersonation role ID. Impersonation role ID can only be used with Action EWS.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceGetDefaultRetentionPolicy.getDefaultRetentionPolicy
tools.description: Gets the default retention policy details for the specified organization.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceGetImpersonationRole.getImpersonationRole
tools.description: Gets the impersonation role details for the given WorkMail organization.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceGetImpersonationRoleEffect.getImpersonationRoleEffect
tools.description: Tests whether the given impersonation role can impersonate a target user.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceGetMailboxDetails.getMailboxDetails
tools.description: Requests a user's mailbox details for a specified organization and user.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceGetMailDomain.getMailDomain
tools.description: Gets details for a mail domain, including domain records required to configure your domain with recommended security.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceGetMobileDeviceAccessEffect.getMobileDeviceAccessEffect
tools.description: Simulates the effect of the mobile device access rules for the given attributes of a sample access event. Use this method to test the effects of the current set of mobile device access rules for the WorkMail organization for a particular user's attributes.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceGetMobileDeviceAccessOverride.getMobileDeviceAccessOverride
tools.description: Gets the mobile device access override for the given WorkMail organization, user, and device.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceListAccessControlRules.listAccessControlRules
tools.description: Lists the access control rules for the specified organization.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceListAliases.listAliases
tools.description: Creates a paginated call to list the aliases associated with a given entity.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceListAvailabilityConfigurations.listAvailabilityConfigurations
tools.description: List all the AvailabilityConfiguration's for the given WorkMail organization.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceListGroupMembers.listGroupMembers
tools.description: Returns an overview of the members of a group. Users and groups can be members of a group.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceListGroups.listGroups
tools.description: Returns summaries of the organization's groups.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceListImpersonationRoles.listImpersonationRoles
tools.description: Lists all the impersonation roles for the given WorkMail organization.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceListMailboxExportJobs.listMailboxExportJobs
tools.description: Lists the mailbox export jobs started for the specified organization within the last seven days.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceListMailboxPermissions.listMailboxPermissions
tools.description: Lists the mailbox permissions associated with a user, group, or resource mailbox.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceListMailDomains.listMailDomains
tools.description: Lists the mail domains in a given WorkMail organization.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceListMobileDeviceAccessOverrides.listMobileDeviceAccessOverrides
tools.description: Lists all the mobile device access overrides for any given combination of WorkMail organization, user, or device.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceListMobileDeviceAccessRules.listMobileDeviceAccessRules
tools.description: Lists the mobile device access rules for the specified WorkMail organization.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceListOrganizations.listOrganizations
tools.description: Returns summaries of the customer's organizations.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceListResourceDelegates.listResourceDelegates
tools.description: Lists the delegates associated with a resource. Users and groups can be resource delegates and answer requests on behalf of the resource.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceListResources.listResources
tools.description: Returns summaries of the organization's resources.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceListTagsForResource.listTagsForResource
tools.description: Lists the tags applied to an WorkMail organization resource.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceListUsers.listUsers
tools.description: Returns summaries of the organization's users.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServicePutAccessControlRule.putAccessControlRule
tools.description: Adds a new access control rule for the specified organization. The rule allows or denies access to the organization for the specified IPv4 addresses, access protocol actions, user IDs and impersonation IDs. Adding a new rule with the same name as an existing rule replaces the older rule.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServicePutEmailMonitoringConfiguration.putEmailMonitoringConfiguration
tools.description: Creates or updates the email monitoring configuration for a specified organization.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServicePutInboundDmarcSettings.putInboundDmarcSettings
tools.description: Enables or disables a DMARC policy for a given organization.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServicePutMailboxPermissions.putMailboxPermissions
tools.description: Sets permissions for a user, group, or resource. This replaces any pre-existing permissions.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServicePutMobileDeviceAccessOverride.putMobileDeviceAccessOverride
tools.description: Creates or updates a mobile device access override for the given WorkMail organization, user, and device.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServicePutRetentionPolicy.putRetentionPolicy
tools.description: Puts a retention policy to the specified organization.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceRegisterMailDomain.registerMailDomain
tools.description: Registers a new domain in WorkMail and SES, and configures it for use by WorkMail. Emails received by SES for this domain are routed to the specified WorkMail organization, and WorkMail has permanent permission to use the specified domain for sending your users' emails.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceRegisterToWorkMail.registerToWorkMail
tools.description:
Registers an existing and disabled user, group, or resource for WorkMail use by associating a mailbox and calendaring capabilities. It performs no change if the user, group, or resource is enabled and fails if the user, group, or resource is deleted. This operation results in the accumulation of costs. For more information, see Pricing. The equivalent console functionality for this operation is Enable.

Users can either be created by calling the CreateUser API operation or they can be synchronized from your directory. For more information, see DeregisterFromWorkMail.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceResetPassword.resetPassword
tools.description: Allows the administrator to reset the password for a user.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceStartMailboxExportJob.startMailboxExportJob
tools.description: Starts a mailbox export job to export MIME-format email messages and calendar items from the specified mailbox to the specified Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) bucket. For more information, see Exporting mailbox content in the WorkMail Administrator Guide.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceTagResource.tagResource
tools.description: Applies the specified tags to the specified WorkMailorganization resource.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceTestAvailabilityConfiguration.testAvailabilityConfiguration
tools.description:
Performs a test on an availability provider to ensure that access is allowed. For EWS, it verifies the provided credentials can be used to successfully log in. For Lambda, it verifies that the Lambda function can be invoked and that the resource access policy was configured to deny anonymous access. An anonymous invocation is one done without providing either a SourceArn or SourceAccount header.

The request must contain either one provider definition (EwsProvider or LambdaProvider) or the DomainName parameter. If the DomainName parameter is provided, the configuration stored under the DomainName will be tested.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceUntagResource.untagResource
tools.description: Untags the specified tags from the specified WorkMail organization resource.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceUpdateAvailabilityConfiguration.updateAvailabilityConfiguration
tools.description: Updates an existing AvailabilityConfiguration for the given WorkMail organization and domain.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceUpdateDefaultMailDomain.updateDefaultMailDomain
tools.description: Updates the default mail domain for an organization. The default mail domain is used by the WorkMail AWS Console to suggest an email address when enabling a mail user. You can only have one default domain.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceUpdateImpersonationRole.updateImpersonationRole
tools.description: Updates an impersonation role for the given WorkMail organization.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceUpdateMailboxQuota.updateMailboxQuota
tools.description: Updates a user's current mailbox quota for a specified organization and user.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceUpdateMobileDeviceAccessRule.updateMobileDeviceAccessRule
tools.description: Updates a mobile device access rule for the specified WorkMail organization.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceUpdatePrimaryEmailAddress.updatePrimaryEmailAddress
tools.description: Updates the primary email for a user, group, or resource. The current email is moved into the list of aliases (or swapped between an existing alias and the current primary email), and the email provided in the input is promoted as the primary.

tools.name: xAmzTargetWorkMailServiceUpdateResource.updateResource
tools.description: Updates data for the resource. To have the latest information, it must be preceded by a DescribeResource call. The dataset in the request should be the one expected when performing another DescribeResource call.

tools.name: openapi.previewSpec
tools.description: Preview an OpenAPI document before adding it as a source

tools.name: openapi.addSource
tools.description: Add an OpenAPI source and register its operations as tools
